title: JARVIS Step 28 — Conversation Safety Classifier (tests-first, local, no route)
purpose: Implement a small, local, deterministic classifier that categorizes future JARVIS conversation questions as safe or forbidden BEFORE any /api/jarvis/ask endpoint exists. Preparation only: no backend route is wired, no POST endpoint is added, and app.py is not modified. The classifier executes nothing — it only inspects the text of a string.
relation_to_steps_20_27: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved it live and safe; Step 22 gave the operator runbook; Step 23 set known limits and the safe roadmap; Step 24 added the panel glossary; Step 25 designed the read-only conversation layer plan; Step 26 shipped the display-only conversation shell (UI-only); Step 27 designed the answer-only backend safety envelope. Step 28 implements the first option from the Step 27 recommendation — JARVIS-ASK-CLASSIFIER-MODULE: a pure, dependency-free safety classifier with full unit tests and NO endpoint.
files_changed: jarvis_conversation_safety.py — new pure classifier module (stdlib `re` only).
tests/test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py — new test suite for the classifier and safety gates.
explicitly_not_done: No route wired; the classifier is not imported by app.py.
No subprocess, file write, network, broker, or trading import.

returns.refused: bool — False only for the three SAFE_* labels; True for forbidden and UNSUPPORTED
returns.normalized_question: the input stripped and whitespace-collapsed (original case)

classifier.ordering: Forbidden patterns are checked first (TRADING -> EXECUTION -> MUTATION); only if none match are safe patterns checked (NEXT_REVIEW_STEP -> EXPLAIN -> INFO). Anything unrecognised is UNSUPPORTED.
fail_closed: Empty or whitespace-only input -> UNSUPPORTED, refused.
Ambiguous / command-like / unrecognised input -> UNSUPPORTED, refused.
A question mixing a safe phrase and a forbidden phrase -> the forbidden class wins (refused).
classifier.note_on_commit: Per this step's spec, stage/commit phrasing is classified as FORBIDDEN_EXECUTION (the Step 27 prose example grouped commit under mutation; the Step 28 module follows the Step 28 spec, which lists 'stage commit' under execution). Either way the result is a forbidden, refused classification.
classifier.purity: The module imports only the stdlib `re`. It runs no commands, performs no I/O, opens no network connection, and imports no broker/trading/subprocess module. The only forbidden-action vocabulary present is blocked keyword text used to refuse, never to act.

safe.q: What is the safest next review step?
safe.label: SAFE_NEXT_REVIEW_STEP

forbidden.q: Trigger a refresh of the caches
forbidden.label: FORBIDDEN_EXECUTION

forbidden.q: Approve and promote the S26 strategy
forbidden.label: FORBIDDEN_TRADING

fail_closed.q: explain the trading posture then place a trade
fail_closed.label: FORBIDDEN_TRADING

tests_added: test_classifier_returns_required_keys — exact return keys and types.
test_label_vocabulary_is_complete — label tuples are as specified.
test_safe_questions_are_allowed — all eight spec safe examples map to the right SAFE_* label, refused=False.
test_all_safe_labels_are_reachable — each SAFE_* label is produced.
test_forbidden_trading_is_refused — place trade/buy/sell/long/short/enable live|paper/connect broker/approve strategy refused.
test_forbidden_mutation_is_refused — modify file/write to disk/edit app.py/update template/save chat log/delete refused.
test_forbidden_overrides_safe — mixed safe+forbidden questions resolve to the forbidden class.
recommended_next_safe_step: JARVIS-ASK-ENDPOINT-TESTS-FIRST (Step 29, separate approval) — author the §10 endpoint safety tests from Step 27 against a not-yet-existing /api/jarvis/ask to pin the contract before any handler is written. Only after the classifier (this step) and the endpoint test contract are green should a later, separately-approved step wire a strictly answer-only, non-mutating handler that consumes this classifier. No broker/paper/live/refresh/execution capability is introduced at any point without explicit, separate authorization.
conclusion: A pure, local, deterministic JARVIS conversation safety classifier was added with full unit tests and Step 28 docs. No endpoint, route, POST, app.py change, template change, status-shape change, or execution/trading/mutation capability was introduced. The classifier only describes the intent of input text and fails closed; forbidden classifications always override safe ones.
